scope_classes.subscription_setup: Action remains subscription-scoped in thesis-demo-v1 because it creates resource groups, deployment identities, role assignments, or provider-wide setup resources.
scope_classes.resource_group_candidate: Action should move toward resource-group scope after supervised provider validation confirms exact deployment behavior.
scope_classes.resource_candidate: Action can likely be narrowed to final resource IDs or provider-specific resources in least-privilege-v2.
scope_classes.data_plane: DataAction grants Digital Twins data-plane access and needs separate validation from ARM management-plane actions.
scope_classes.known_gap: Action pattern is intentionally broad or belongs to a bootstrap/deployment split that is not final.

action_groups.name: setup
action_groups.reason: Resource-group creation, ARM deployments, deployment identity setup, and shared storage are bootstrap-level capabilities in thesis-demo-v1.

action_groups.name: observability
action_groups.reason: Terraform creates Log Analytics, Application Insights, and diagnostic settings when Azure logging is enabled.

action_groups.name: function_apps
action_groups.reason: Function deployment, publishing, host-key discovery, and lifecycle operations are required by Azure L0/L1/L2/L3 function resources.

action_groups.name: iot_eventgrid
action_groups.reason: IoT Hub and Event Grid actions support L1 ingestion, routing, and teardown.

action_groups.name: role_assignments
action_groups.reason: Terraform assigns managed identities and users to provider resources; final scoping needs live validation.

action_groups.name: storage_cosmos
action_groups.reason: Cosmos DB and blob container lifecycle actions cover hot/cold/archive storage.

action_groups.name: digital_twins
action_groups.reason: Digital Twins instance and endpoint lifecycle is required for L4.

action_groups.name: grafana
action_groups.reason: Managed Grafana workspace lifecycle is required for L5.

data_action_groups.name: digital_twins_data_plane
data_action_groups.reason: ADT model, twin, relationship, query, and event-route operations are data-plane permissions.

known_gaps: */read remains a broad thesis-demo-v1 read baseline and should be replaced with explicit read actions after provider operation tracing.
Microsoft.Resources/deployments/* is intentionally broad until Logic App ARM-template deployment behavior is isolated.
Azure Resource Manager custom roles do not cover Microsoft Graph permissions required by azuread_user and azuread_* data sources.
E2E deployment verification is intentionally deferred until the larger refactoring roadmap reaches an end-to-end testable state.
next_validation: Run az provider operation list against the active subscription and compare Microsoft.* actions.
Run supervised Azure deployment/destroy smoke with thesis-demo-v1 credentials.
Capture provider-operation evidence to produce least-privilege-v2 with narrower read and deployment actions.
